headline: Iranians express bitterness at war: 'It's all been destruction, and nothing has truly changed'
description: Grappling with record inflation, mass job losses and a harshening of political repression, the current calm feels both fragile and uncertain to many Iranian people.

headline: First non-Iranian oil tanker crosses Strait of Hormuz since ceasefire
Giorgia Meloni and the defeat of ideology
Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter ceasefire
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky had proposed a temporary truce earlier this week. Putin has instructed the Russian General Staff 'to cease combat operations in all directions for this period,' the Kremlin said.

headline: Melania Trump blasts 'lies' linking her to Epstein: 'I am not his victim'
description: Jeffrey Epstein died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges involving minors, but the scandal has repeatedly overshadowed Donald Trump's second presidency. 'I was not a participant. Was never on Epstein's plane, and never visited his private island,' the first lady said.

headline: In Budapest, a seven-hour concert aims to hasten Viktor Orban's 'end of the regime'
description: Robert Puzser, an intellectual and outspoken critic of the Fidesz party led by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, is organizing this historic free show on Friday, April 10, to urge citizens to turn out for Sunday's legislative elections.

headline: Denis Charbit: 'The Israeli lawmakers who established the death penalty merely obeyed their own instincts for vengeance'
description: The professor at the Open University of Israel looks into the recent law introducing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of terrorist acts and anti-Israeli attacks. He sees it as a grave moral regression and is concerned about Israel abandoning 'thousands of years of ethics.'

Mysterious 'knocker' continues haunting elderly women in Brittany town
description: For the past three years in the village of Goudelin, someone has taken pleasure in harassing women living alone, often elderly, by pounding on their doors in the middle of the night. Around 30 victims have been identified. Speculation is rampant about the knocker's identity, and the victims are finally being heard.

headline: Trump's escalatory rhetoric raises questions over his mental health
description: Members of Congress, stunned by the crude and genocidal language used by the president, have begun to discuss the US Constitution's 25th Amendment, which allows for the president to be declared unfit and for executive powers to be transferred to the vice president.

headline: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
description: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.
